preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Ssas trial user for an ai-backed design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is always eligible and sets the session context. For a Mover in the consideration stage, use the minimal variant — strip distracting nav links and surface a single persistent 'Start Free Trial' CTA. Movers don't browse menus; they scan for the fastest path to action.

sections.name: Hero — Single Message, Single Action
sections.rationale: HeroPrimary is eligible for Mover archetype and consideration funnel stage. The Mover variant suppresses subhead and secondary CTA, delivering one clear value proposition and one conversion button above the fold. At the consideration stage, the visitor already knows the category — the hero must crystallize why MODE is the fastest choice, not re-explain the problem space.

sections.name: Silent Credibility Strip
sections.rationale: SocialProofBar is eligible for Mover archetype and consideration funnel stage. Placed directly below the hero, the Mover logos-only variant provides instant, frictionless validation — no headline, no quote, just recognizable brand marks. For a Mover in consideration, this eliminates the 'is this legit?' micro-doubt without slowing momentum.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics
sections.rationale: StatBlock is eligible for Mover archetype and consideration funnel stage. The Mover variant suppresses sources and headlines, presenting 3 pure impact metrics (e.g., '3× faster design iteration', '40% less design-to-dev handoff time'). At the consideration stage, Movers need quantified proof of value — not explanation, just results that confirm their leaning toward action.

sections.name: Mid-Page Conversion Prompt
sections.rationale: CTABanner is eligible for Mover archetype at the decision stage, but it also serves consideration by intercepting Movers who have already seen enough. The Mover variant — strong headline, single CTA, no subhead — acts as an escape hatch for fast deciders mid-scroll. Placing it after the proof-of-value section capitalizes on peak confidence before any friction accumulates.

sections.name: Footer with Recovery CTA
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal is always eligible. The with-cta variant recaptures departing Movers who scrolled to the bottom without converting. At the consideration stage, a final low-friction 'Start your free trial' prompt paired with a trust signal like 'No credit card required' gives the Mover one last frictionless conversion opportunity before exit.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.
Mover variant suppresses subhead and cta_secondary — one message, one action. Text-only aligns with conversion-focused consideration stage for trial conversion.
Mover variant is logos-only, no headline — silent credibility. logos-only is the highest-frequency placement; drop directly below HeroPrimary on awareness pages.
Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact. 3-stat is the preferred default for establishing momentum without requiring trust attribution.
Mover variant: strong headline, single CTA, no subhead. minimal for high-frequency use — above footer, between sections — supports trial conversion focus.
with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Essential for trial conversion recovery on consideration-stage users.
